title: Invalid argument on chown
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Some programs drop privileges to `nobody:nobody` but this fails with EINVAL.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```
$ podman run -it --rm docker.io/alpine
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 188c0c94c7c5 [--------------------------------------] 0.0b / 0.0b
Copying config d6e46aa247 done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
/ # touch batata
/ # ls -ld batata
-rw-r--r--    1 root     root             0 Dec  4 10:53 batata
/ # chown nobody:nobody batata
chown: batata: Invalid argument
/ # id nobody
uid=65534(nobody) gid=65534(nobody) groups=65534(nobody)
/ #
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

I expected the chown call to succeed.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.0.0-dev
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.6
Git Commit:   70284b18cc7d4dc478c439cdda60ba64cfec060b
Built:        Fri Dec  4 10:28:22 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman 2.2.0+rc2+120+g70284b18c-1

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

comments.body: Nobody is a special user used by the kernel to indicate that the UID/GID in question has not been mapped into the rootless user namespace. You cannot use `nobody` directly as a user or group, because it represents many UIDs and GIDs (all of them unusable within the container's user namespace).

I works in Docker 19.03:

```
$ docker run -it --rm docker.io/alpine
/ # touch batata
/ # ls -ld batata
-rw-r--r--    1 root     root             0 Dec  4 16:49 batata
/ # chown nobody:nobody batata
/ # ls -ld batata
-rw-r--r--    1 nobody   nobody           0 Dec  4 16:49 batata
/ # id nobody
uid=65534(nobody) gid=65534(nobody) groups=65534(nobody)
```

And outside Docker:
```
$ ll
total 8.0K
-rw-r--r-- 1 rzl rzl 260 Dec  3 14:03 Dockerfile
-rw-r--r-- 1 rzl rzl 155 Dec  3 14:04 supervisord.conf
$ touch batata
$ ls -ld batata
-rw-r--r-- 1 rzl rzl 0 Dec  4 16:51 batata
$ sudo chown nobody:nobody batata
$ ls -ld batata
-rw-r--r-- 1 nobody nobody 0 Dec  4 16:51 batata
$ id nobody
uid=65534(nobody) gid=65534(nobody) groups=65534(nobody)
```

As far as I understand, `nobody` is a regular user. (Docker is mapping it to `nobody` on the host.) It seems uid -1 is indeed used as the invalid uid but I believe we are not hitting that case, since uids aren't 16-bit, at least on my machine. This is not a big issue: I can work around this by telling the program (`php-fpm`, in this case) to use another user. I am filing this issue because, in its default configuration on Alpine Linux, php-fpm will not start because it defaults to `setuid(nobody)`. It seems something that should work.
Have you granted user enough subordinate user ids to allow podman to allocate uid 65534?

Good point! I tacked another 0 at the end of my `/etc/subuid` (and gid) and now it works:

```
rzl:100000:100000
```

@zhangguanzhang: Closing this issue.

<details>

In response to [this](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/8592#issuecomment-739150358):

>/close


Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>
